texto: Each of us promenades his thought, like a monkey on a leash. When you read, you always have to such monkeys: your own and one belonging to someone else. Or, even worse, a monkey and a hyena. Now, consider what you will feed them. For a hyena does not eat the same things as a monkey...
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Milorad Pavić: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Each of us promenades his thought, like a monkey on a leash. When you read, you always have to su…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, reading, monkey pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: Houses are like books: so many of them around you, yet you only look at a few and visit or reside in fewer still.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Milorad Pavić: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Houses are like books: so many of them around you, yet you only look at a few and visit or reside…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, novel, story pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de story.

frase_original: Houses are like books: so many of them around you, yet you only look at a few and visit or reside in fewer still.

texto: And so, when I began to read the proffered pages, I at one moment lost the train of thought in the text and drowned it in my own feelings. In these seconds of absence and self-oblivion, centuries passed with every read but uncomprehended and unabsorbed line, and when, after a few moments, I came to and re-established contact with the text, I knew that the reader who returns from the open seas of his feelings is no longer the same reader who embarked on that sea only a short while ago.
explicacao: Citação de Milorad Pavić sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “And so, when I began to read the proffered pages, I at one moment lost the train of thought in th…” articula tempo, dictionary, khazar em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e termino.

frase_original: And so, when I began to read the proffered pages, I at one moment lost the train of thought in the text and drowned it in my own feelings. In these seconds of absence and self-oblivion, centuries passed with every read but uncomprehended and unabsorbed line, and when, after a few moments, I came to and re-established contact with the text, I knew that the reader who returns from the open seas of his feelings is no longer the same reader who embarked on that sea only a short while ago.
